title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip and Mary Darland discuss their shared alibi, which involves claiming they were playing cards during the time of the murder to avoid suspicion. Philip expresses a deep curiosity regarding the killer's identity, noting that Mickey believes the murderer is a member of their group. While Mary remains indifferent and wishes to maintain a sense of normalcy, Philip argues that the situation is deteriorating. He points to Dr. Donald’s nervous behavior toward Hester and the potential motives surrounding the upcoming marriage of Leo and Gwendolyn.

Mary suggests that Custon, an outsider who has been suffering from headaches, might be the culprit. Philip observes that Custon is in a precarious position because she is alone and cannot prove her innocence regarding the death of her friend and employer. Philip proposes investigating the truth by observing how people react to specific statements, but Mary urges him to stop setting traps and to mind his own business.

Meanwhile, Michael Ainsley lies awake in bed, reflecting on his past life in the London slums. He reminisces about his mother’s drinking habits and the excitement of the war and air raids. He compares his current life in the countryside to his former home, feeling like a prisoner subjected to bland food. He expresses resentment toward a woman who took him in and treats him with "drivel" and games, but he remains patient, hoping to eventually return to London.
